per_session: session_profiles (SessionProfileRecord) - the profile digest for one session
session_work_events - work-event timeline derived from that session's messages/blocks
session_phases - phase segmentation of one session
session_runs (run projection) - execution runs within one session
session_observed_events - tool_finished/command outcomes from that session's keystone tool-result fields
session_context_snapshots - context-boundary snapshots for one session
logical_session_id resolution - resolved root of the parent chain; depends on topology_edges/session_links but is a per-session lookup (thread_root_ids_sync), NOT a multi-session recompute
cross_session: threads + thread_sessions - group all sessions sharing a root; adding a child mutates its root's thread membership/depth/session_count
session_tag_rollups - tag aggregates across sessions in a provider-day
provider_day aggregates / day summaries - group by (origin, day) (refresh_sync_provider_day_aggregates over profile_provider_day groups)
week/archive rollups + logical_session_count reducers (insights/archive_summaries.py, storage/insights/aggregate/) - higher rollups over day aggregates

rationale: The insights work is a derived, rebuildable read model over index.db, not stored meaning. Three concrete, live-observed forces make it wrong to fold into the ingest write transaction (commit_archive_write_effects) but right to keep as an automatic convergence invariant: (1) WAL/lock isolation - the per-session build hydrates the full transcript and runs CPU-bound analysis; a full rebuild once ran as ONE transaction and grew a ~6 GB WAL holding a minutes-long index.db write lock (bead 1xc.1 / commit 2eee22a9f). The fix was message-budget chunking with per-chunk commits. Folding the build into the ingest write commit would re-enlarge that transaction, hold the write lock through the analysis, and defeat the chunking that exists specifically to bound the WAL. (2) Hot-session churn batching - live-append sources (Claude Code JSONL growth, browser capture) re-ingest the same session many times; make_insights_stage defers 'hot' sessions for a 60s quiet window (_HOT_INSIGHT_QUIET_SECONDS) so the expensive profile is built once after churn settles instead of on every append. Inline-in-write would rebuild N times. (3) Rebuild-after-schema-bump decoupling - insights carry SESSION_INSIGHT_MATERIALIZER_VERSION and the stage's staleness check compares it; when the materializer or index tier version bumps, ALL profiles must rebuild from index rows WITHOUT re-ingesting source. content_hash idempotency means a same-hash re-ingest skips the write entirely, so an inline-only design would force a full source re-ingest just to refresh derived insights. A standalone, separately-drivable rebuild entrypoint is required. CRUCIALLY: the cross-session half is already incremental-on-write, not a deferred global pass - the incremental path (session_ids given) refreshes only affected thread roots (thread_root_ids_sync on the changed sessions) and only affected provider-day groups (previous_profile_groups | refreshed_profile_groups). A global thread/tag/day recompute happens ONLY on the full rebuild (session_ids=None), i.e. the schema-bump path, which is inherently global. So the operator's instinct is half-right: there is no 'weird deferred cross-session pass' in steady state to eliminate; what exists is a scoped incremental refresh already. The real defect the operator is sensing is framing, not architecture - the insights stage is a peer of the fts and embed convergence stages (make_default_convergence_stages returns exactly those three) but is under-hardened relative to them: unlike fts (line 248) and embed (line 309), the insights ConvergenceStage does NOT set false_means_pending=True, so a crash mid-rebuild records FAILED and strands the archive at partial profiles (observed live: 395/16398) instead of being re-driven. That, plus the giant-single-session unbounded build (1xc.6), is why it feels like a fragile bolt-on rather than an automatic invariant.
recommendation: Do NOT dissolve insights into the ingest write transaction. Per-session-inline in commit_archive_write_effects is the wrong move: it breaks materializer-version rebuild (would force source re-ingest), defeats the message-budget WAL chunking, holds the index.db write lock through CPU-bound analysis, and re-builds hot sessions on every append. Instead, complete the reframing the operator is reaching for: keep insights as a SINGLE automatic convergence invariant on par with FTS and embeddings - a separately-drivable derived-view refresh that the daemon (and the pipeline 'materialize' stage) trigger automatically, never a manual operator step (aligns with the 'automagic-invariants' memory). Concretely: (per-session-only fold is unnecessary and harmful; not-at-all fold into the write txn). The high-value work is hardening the existing stage to true peer status: (a) 1xc.4 - add false_means_pending=True so partial rebuilds are retried, not stranded FAILED; (b) 1xc.1 - land the regression test proving per-chunk commit / mid-rebuild visibility; (c) 1xc.6 - bound per-session build for giant sessions. The cross-session aggregates are ALREADY incremental-on-affected-scope, so no deferred global pass needs removing - only the schema-bump full rebuild stays global, correctly. Optionally, the operator's deeper 'insights as declared derived views' intuition is a valid longer-horizon refactor (a single declared-view refresh engine over profiles+aggregates+threads) but there is NO existing bead for it - it should be filed rather than conflated with the near-term hardening.
existing_beads: polylogue-1xc (P1 epic) - Scale-hardening: bugs that only bite on real-scale archives; parent of the insights-stage hardening children

polylogue-1xc.1 (P1 bug) - Regression-guard chunked insight rebuild against single-transaction WAL blowup; implementation SHIPPED (2eee22a9f), residual = executable regression test proving >1 commit boundary + mid-rebuild visibility

polylogue-1xc.4 (P1 bug) - Make insights convergence stage resumable and per-session idempotent on crash; add false_means_pending=True (parity with fts line 248 / embed line 309); THIS is the core of the operator's 'weird separate stage' complaint
polylogue-1xc.6 (P2 bug) - Bound per-session insight build cost for giant sessions (9-min-batch pathology); extend the existing heavy_session/degraded-mode path to actually cap/stream
polylogue-1xc.3 (P1 bug) - Auto-drain raw-materialization debt via convergence stage; sibling automagic-invariant work for the source->index leg
polylogue-1xc.7 (P2 task) - Seeded large-archive scale-regression lane; the harness the above regression tests plug into
note_on_referenced_beads: The bead ids named in the task (5wp 'Insights as declared derived views', yp0 'event bus', iec 'schema optimization') do NOT exist in this Beads workspace - they were hypothetical examples in the prompt. No live bead proposes dissolving/streamlining the insights stage into declared views or an event bus; the closest live work is the 1xc scale-hardening family which hardens the existing stage rather than restructuring it. The 'insights-as-declared-views' idea is therefore an unfiled gap, proposed below.

proposed_beads.title: Reframe insights as a first-class convergence invariant (peer of fts/embed), not a bolt-on stage
proposed_beads.design: The operator's audit conclusion: the insights ConvergenceStage should read as one of three automatic derived-model invariants (fts, embed, insights) that the daemon enforces, never a manual/optional step. Today make_insights_stage lacks false_means_pending=True (unlike fts@248/embed@309), so a partial rebuild is stranded FAILED (observed 395/16398 live). This bead is the umbrella that sequences 1xc.4 (resumability), 1xc.1 (regression proof), 1xc.6 (giant-session bound), and a docs pass: docs/internals.md should describe insights refresh as an automagic convergence invariant with the same guarantees as FTS coherence, and remove any framing that suggests it is optional operator maintenance. Do NOT fold per-session build into commit_archive_write_effects - preserve WAL-chunked, hot-quiet-window, materializer-version-rebuildable behavior. Files: polylogue/daemon/convergence_stages.py (make_insights_stage), docs/internals.md.
proposed_beads.acceptance: 1) make_insights_stage sets false_means_pending=True and passes the 1xc.4 resumability test. 2) docs/internals.md documents insights as a convergence invariant peer to fts/embed with identical resumability/idempotency guarantees and states the three reasons it is NOT inlined into the write transaction (WAL/lock isolation, hot-churn batching, materializer-version rebuild). 3) No new manual-only insight maintenance CLI surface is introduced. Verify: devtools test tests/unit/daemon (insights stage tests) + devtools render all --check.

proposed_beads.title: Design spike: express session insights + aggregates as declared derived views over a single refresh engine
proposed_beads.design: Longer-horizon refactor the operator gestured at ('insights as declared derived views'). Today per-session (profiles, latency, work_events, phases, runs, observed_events, context_snapshots) and cross-session (threads, session_tag_rollups, provider_day aggregates) refresh logic is hand-woven across rebuild.py (~1600 lines), aggregates.py, threads.py, and the convergence stage. Evaluate whether these can be declared as a registry of derived-view specs (source rows -> materialized table, per-session vs grouped scope, materializer version) driven by one incremental refresh engine that automatically computes the affected scope on write and the global scope on version bump. Goal: collapse the bespoke incremental-vs-full branching and make adding an insight a declaration rather than editing five files. This is a spike/ADR, NOT a commitment to rewrite - measure whether the abstraction pays for itself against the current working code. Cross-reference insights/registry.py (already a partial registry).
proposed_beads.acceptance: 1) An ADR under docs/ (or thoughtspace) that inventories every current insight table, classifies per-session vs cross-session scope and its affected-scope function, and proposes (or explicitly rejects) a declared-derived-view registry with a single refresh engine. 2) Includes a migration sketch and a cost/benefit call vs leaving rebuild.py as-is. 3) If accepted, spawns implementation child beads; if rejected, records why so it is not re-litigated. No production code change in this bead.
